content: Kavya__________go to work yesterday as she________ feeling well. Select the most approrpriate option to complete the above sentence.
explanation:
The best choice is D:

“Kavya couldn’t go to work yesterday as she wasn’t feeling well.”

Explanation:

“couldn’t” correctly expresses that she was unable to go.

“wasn’t feeling well” matches the past-time setting (“yesterday”) and explains the reason.

content:
Good : Evil : : Genuine : ?

Select the most appropriate option to complete the analogy.
explanation:
The pair “Good : Evil” is one of direct opposites. In the same way, the opposite of “Genuine” is “Counterfeit.”

Answer: Option A (Counterfeit)

content:
If ‘→’ denotes increasing order of intensity, then the meaning of the words [talk → shout → scream] is analogous to [please → _______ → pander].
Which one of the given options is appropriate to fill the blank?
explanation:
The correct answer is Option A: flatter.

Here's why:

The analogy is based on increasing intensity of action.

  • talk → shout → scream: This sequence shows an escalation from a quiet conversation to a loud, forceful expression.
  • please → flatter → pander: This sequence also illustrates increasing intensity in terms of persuasion or trying to get someone to do something.

Let's analyze the options:

  • flatter: To praise someone excessively, often insincerely, to win favor. This fits the increasing intensity of persuasion, moving beyond a simple "please" to a more calculated attempt to influence.
  • flutter: To move quickly and lightly. This doesn't relate to the concept of persuasion or increasing intensity.
  • fritter: To waste time or money. This doesn't fit the context of trying to influence someone.
  • frizzle: To curl or crisp, usually referring to hair. This is completely unrelated to the analogy.

Therefore, flatter is the only word that maintains the increasing intensity of the analogy, making it the most appropriate choice to fill the blank.

content:
Rafi told Mary, "I am thinking of watching a film this weekend."

The following repots the above statement in indirect speech:

Rafi told Mary that he _________ of watching a film that weekend.
explanation:
The correct answer is (d) was thinking.

When reporting someone else's statement in indirect or reported speech, the tense of the reported verb usually changes. In this case, since the original statement was made in the present continuous tense ("I am thinking of watching a film this weekend"), the reported speech should use the past continuous tense ("he was thinking of watching a film that weekend").

Therefore, the correct way to report Rafi's statement in indirect speech is :

"Rafi told Mary that he was thinking of watching a film that weekend."

content:
Permit : __________ : : Enforce : Relax

(By word meaning)

A recent survey shows that 65% of tobacco users were advised to stop consuming tobacco. The survey also shows that 3 out of 10 tobacco users attempted to stop using tobacco.

Based only on the information in the above passage, which one of the following options can be logically inferred with certainty?

options.content: A majority of tobacco users who were advised to stop consuming tobacco made an attempt to do so.
A majority of tobacco users who were advised to stop consuming tobacco did not attempt to do so.
Approximately 30% of tobacco users successfully stopped consuming tobacco.
Approximately 65% of tobacco users successfully stopped consuming tobacco.

explanation: Option (b) A majority of tobacco users who were advised to stop consuming tobacco did not attempt to do so can be logically inferred with certainty based only on the information in the above passage.

content:
Which one of the options does NOT describe the passage below or follow from it?

We tend to think of cancer as a 'modern' illness because its metaphors are so modern. It is a disease of overproduction, of sudden growth, a growth that is unstoppable, tipped into the abyss of no control. Modern cell biology encourages us to imagine the cell as a molecular machine. Cancer is that machine unable to quench its initial command (to grow) and thus transform into an indestructible, self-propelled automaton.

[Adapted from The Emperor of All Maladies by Siddhartha Mukherjee]

options.content: It is a reflection of why cancer seems so modern to most of us.
It tells us that modern cell biology uses and promotes metaphors of machinery.
Modern cell biology encourages metaphors of machinery, and cancer is often imagined as a machine.
Modern cell biology never uses figurative language, such as metaphors, to describe or explain anything.

explanation:
The option that does NOT describe the passage or follow from it is (d) Modern cell biology never uses figurative language, such as metaphors, to describe or explain anything.

The passage discusses the metaphors used to describe cancer as a modern disease of overproduction and sudden growth, and how modern cell biology encourages us to imagine the cell as a molecular machine. Therefore, options (a), (b), and (c) all describe or follow from the passage. Option (d), on the other hand, contradicts the passage as it suggests that modern cell biology never uses figurative language, which is not supported by the passage.

content: As you grow older, an injury to your __________ may take longer to ___________.

Given below are two statements and four conclusions drawn based on the statements.

Statement I : Some bottles are cups.

Statement II : All cups are knives.

Conclusion I : Some bottles are knives.

Conclusion II : Some knives are cups.

Conclusion III : All cups are bottles.

Conclusion IV : All knives are cups.

Which one of the following options can be logically inferred?

options.content: Only conclusion I and conclusion II are correct.
Only conclusion II and conclusion III are correct.
Only conclusion II and conclusion IV are correct.
Only conclusion III and conclusion IV are correct.

explanation: Conclusions I and II are correct.

content: The people $\_\_\_\_$ were at the demonstration were from all sections of society.
explanation:
Let's look at the sentence:

The people _ were at the demonstration were from all sections of society.

We need a relative pronoun that refers to "the people."

  • A. whose → shows possession; doesn’t fit here.

    Example: The people whose car broke down… (not appropriate here)

  • B. which → refers to things, not people.

    Example: The car which was red…

  • C. who → refers to people and acts as the subject of the relative clause.

    ✅ The people who were at the demonstration…

  • D. whom → refers to people but as the object, not the subject.

    Example: The people whom we met…

✅ Correct answer: C. who

Final sentence:

The people who were at the demonstration were from all sections of society.

content:
Oasis is to sand as island is to $\_\_\_\_$ .

Which one of the following options maintains a similar logical relation in the above sentence?
explanation:
Oasis is a fertile land surrounded by sand.

Island is a fertile land surrounded by water.

Hence, the correct option is (C).

many of them.
The passengers were angry __________ the airline staff about the delay.

"A common misconception among writers is that sentence structure mirrors thought; the more _________ the structure, the more complicated the ideas."

The word that best fills the blank in the above sentence is

"Since you have gone off the ________, the _________ sand is likely to damage the car."
The words that best fill the blanks in the above sentence are

Identify the option that has the most appropriate sequence such that a coherent paragraph is formed :

(P) It is because deer, like most of the animals that tigers normally prey on, run much faster! It simply means, another day of empty stomach for the big cats.

(Q) Tigers spend most of their life searching for food.

(R) If they trace the scent of deer, tigers follow the trail, chase the deer for a mile or two in the dark, and yet may not catch them.

(S) For several nights, they relentlessely prowl through the forest, hunting for a trail that may lead to their prey.
explanation:
The most coherent ordering is

Q → S → R → P

So the correct choice is Option C.

content:
Spheres of unit diameter are centered at ( $l, m, n$ ) where $l, m$ and $n$ take every possible integer values.

The distance between two spheres is computed from the center of one sphere to the center of the other sphere. For a given sphere, $x$ is the distance to its nearest sphere and $y$ is the distance to its next nearest sphere. The value of $\frac{y}{x}$ is :

In the given text, the blanks are numbered (i)–(iv). Select the best match for all the blanks.

Following a row ____(i) ______ the shopkeeper ____(ii) ______ the price of a frying pan, the cook stood ____(iii) ______ a row to withdraw cash _____(iv) ______ the ATM booth.
explanation:
The correct answer is Option C:

(i) with (ii) over (iii) in (iv) at

Here's a breakdown of why each option is the best fit:

(i) "with" is the most suitable preposition here. It signifies the cook's involvement in the row.

(ii) "over" is the best choice. It implies the shopkeeper was arguing or discussing the price with the cook.

(iii) "in" is the appropriate preposition as it indicates the cook's location in the row.

(iv) "at" is the correct preposition for describing the location of the ATM booth.

Therefore, the complete sentence reads: "Following a row with the shopkeeper over the price of a frying pan, the cook stood in a row to withdraw cash at the ATM booth."

content: Altruism is the human concern for the well being of others. Altruism has been shown to be motivated more by social bonding, familiarity and identification of belongingness to a group. The notion that altruism may be attributed to empathy or guilt has now been rejected. Which one of the following is the CORRECT logical inference based on the information in the above passage?

options.content: Humans engage in altruism due to guilt but not empathy.
Humans engage in altruism due to empathy but not guilt.
Humans engage in altruism due to group identification but not empathy.
Humans  engage in altruism due to empathy but not familiarity.

explanation:
Based on the information in the passage, the correct logical inference is:

Option C: Humans engage in altruism due to group identification but not empathy.

The passage clearly states that altruism is motivated more by social bonding, familiarity, and identification with a group, and it mentions that the notion of altruism being motivated by empathy or guilt has been rejected. Therefore, option C appropriately captures this idea by highlighting that group identification (a form of social bonding or familiarity) is a key motivator, while empathy is not.

content:
The importance of sleep is often overlooked by students when they are preparing for exams. Research has consistently shown that sleep deprivation greatly reduces the ability to recall the material learnt. Hence, cutting down on sleep to study longer hours can be counterproductive.

Which one of the following statements is the CORRECT inference from the above passage?

options.content: Sleeping well alone is enough to prepare for an exam. Studying has lesser benefit.
Students are efficient and are not wrong in thinking that sleep is a waste of time.
If a student is extremely well prepared for an exam, he needs little or no sleep.
To do well in an exam, adequate sleep must be part of the preparation.

explanation:
The passage says that:

  • Students often cut down on sleep while studying.

  • Research shows that sleep deprivation reduces the ability to recall learned material.

  • Therefore, studying longer hours at the cost of sleep can be counterproductive.

From these points, the logical inference is that adequate sleep helps improve exam performance and should be part of exam preparation.

✅ Correct answer: Option D — “To do well in an exam, adequate sleep must be part of the preparation.”

content:
An award-winning study by a group of researchers suggests that men are as prone to buying on impulse as women but women feel more guilty about shopping.

Which one of the following statements can be inferred from the given text?

options.content: Some men and women indulge in buying on impulse
All men and women indulge in buying on impulse
Few men and women indulge in buying on impulse
Many men and women indulge in buying on impulse

content:

1. Lakshmi is taller than Rekha

2. Rekha is shorter than Mita

3. Rekha is taller than Sana

4. Sana is shorter than Ganga
